current.summary: Capability monitor panel round 1 review complete; Remy identified 6 concerns with selector epistemology gap as priority

activeWork.description: Analyzing capability monitor panel feedback from six panelists (round 1 of review cycle)
activeWork.progress: All six panelist entries validated and appended to capmon-panel-bus.jsonl; Remy's round 1 concerns documented; primary blocker identified: selector epistemology gap (cannot distinguish unchanged data from selector still matching same node)

recentDecisions: Selector epistemology is the critical blocking issue in Remy's feedback (D12, D13)
Panel review uses JSONL append pattern for accumulating round-by-round feedback
Capability monitor design requires addressing all six concerns before implementation proceeds
nextSteps: Address Remy's 6 concerns, prioritizing selector epistemology gap (D12, D13)
Design solution for selector state tracking that distinguishes unchanged data from selector re-matching
Iterate with panel feedback if needed before capability monitor implementation

context.notes: Capability monitor is a cross-provider feature requiring panel-based review. Remy's round 1 feedback identified a fundamental design gap: the selector cannot distinguish between data that hasn't changed vs. a selector that re-matched the same node. This is a blocking issue for capability monitor semantics. The six concerns are in capmon-panel-bus.jsonl (line 6). Next work focuses on resolving the selector epistemology problem.
